| PARTIAL | 15 |
| MISSING | 9 |
| UNCLEAR | 0 |
| **Total** | **29** |

MET: Agent #40, Tool #64, Tool #58, Agent #36, Tool #59

## Prioritized punch list

Ordered by how much closing each item would move the needle, not by tag number.

1. **No mechanical enforcement of the sovereign-tooling mandate (Tool #60, PARTIAL).** CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md declare raw `wrangler deploy` / `git commit` / `pnpm install` "TASK FAILURE," but `.husky/pre-commit`/`pre-push` gate other invariants (no-sorry-in-Lean, no-raw-react) while nothing blocks these commands, and a since-disabled cloud routine already exploited exactly this gap in production. *Recommendation:* add a PreToolUse hook (or husky pre-push scan) that rejects raw `wrangler deploy`, `git add -A`/`git commit` outside `a0 gn flow`, and raw `pnpm install`, mirroring the existing raw-react/raw-three gates.

2. **The actual LLM-agent tool-calling runtime has no journal at all (Runtime #41 + Runtime #43, both PARTIAL).** `.a0/control-plane/events.jsonl` is real and structured but covers only the a0 build/typecheck task graph; `open-source/gnosis/distributed-inference-host/src/agentic-chat.ts` has zero `console./logger./writeLog` calls, and `.claude/settings.json` has no PostToolUse hook capturing tool-call records either. *Recommendation:* emit `mcp.tool.call`/`mcp.tool.result` events (tool, args, result summary, timestamp, sequence) from `buildToolContent`/`callLocalTool` in `apps/mcp-superserver/src/runtime.ts` into an equivalent journal, and/or add a PostToolUse hook so agent-level runs are reconstructable independent of any single host's session transcript.

3. **No unified operator alert channel (Operator #25, MISSING).** What exists is the "five dashboards" anti-pattern: `apps/cli/src/admin/commands/costs.ts` requires a manual pull, `shared-backend/src/services/email/templates/costAlert.ts` is customer-facing SaaS billing, and `open-source/a0/src/diagnose.ts` writes to `.a0/rejections.jsonl` for an automated repair loop, not a human. Two real incidents (a ~$2k Cloud Run bill, a ~1000-browser-hour CF Browser Rendering burn) were both discovered reactively. *Recommendation:* build one aggregation point (scheduled `a0` task via the `schedule`/RemoteTrigger substrate) that polls Cloudflare + GCP billing + `rejections.jsonl` and posts a single consolidated alert, starting with email via the existing service.

4. **No scheduled weekly cost/security digest (Operator #28, MISSING).** `open-source/a0/src/cloudflare-cost.ts` (`a0 audit cloudflare`) is a real but manually-invoked, cost-only estimator; no weekly/digest/nightly summary generator exists anywhere in `open-source/a0/src`, `open-source/gnosis/src`, or `apps/mcp-superserver/src`. *Recommendation:* use the `schedule` skill/RemoteTrigger to run `a0 audit cloudflare` plus a security-events pull weekly, posted through the channel built in item 3 — do not resurrect the disabled grinder routines for this.

5. **No off-terminal delivery for whatever alert channel exists (Operator #29, MISSING).** No pushover/SMS/SES wiring tied to security or cost events was found; the only scheduling substrate capable of delivering one (cloud routines) was disabled precisely because it was misused for compute grinding. *Recommendation:* once item 3's channel exists, deliver it via the same `schedule`/RemoteTrigger substrate on a cron that emails/pushes the operator directly.

6. **No login/account-access anomaly detection for the operator's own provider accounts (Operator #27, MISSING).** The only "access log" in the repo (`shared-utils/src/community/data/accessLog.ts`) is an in-product end-user feature, unrelated to Cloudflare/GitHub/npm account security. *Recommendation:* add a scheduled check polling each provider's audit-log API for logins/API-key use from unrecognized IPs, piping hits into the item-3 channel.

7. **No push-based health canary (Operator #31, MISSING).** `forge stats` (per AGENTS.md) is a pull dashboard the operator must query manually; no scheduled workflow or script pages on failure (`.github/workflows` has no canary job; `verify-deployed-mesh.ts`/`scale-mesh.ts` have no alert/page/notify hooks). *Recommendation:* add a scheduled GitHub Actions workflow (or a0 cron) that hits each critical service's `/health` endpoint and fires a push/SMS/email alert (the `PushNotification` tool is already available) on failure.

8. **Truncation/omission signaling is inconsistent across MCP tools (Agent #33 + Tool #57 + Runtime #48, all PARTIAL).** `read_file` in `apps/mcp-superserver/src/runtime.ts` (~line 1138) correctly returns `truncated`, but `list_directory` (~1113-1127) and `search_files` (~1148-1166) silently cap results with no signal at all; `open-source/a0/src/mediate.ts` even has the "... and N more" UI pattern already written (lines 383-386, 658-673) but commented out/dead; and `runner.ts`'s `retainTaskLogLine` silently drops old log lines past a cap with no event recorded. *Recommendation:* lift the `maxBytes`/`truncated` pattern into a shared wrapper in `runtime.ts` so every tool result gets it by default, re-enable the dead "and N more" lines in `mediate.ts`, and add a truncated-count event when `retainTaskLogLine` drops lines.

9. **No validated output contract for MCP tool results (Agent #34, PARTIAL).** `buildToolContent` (`runtime.ts:999-1011`) returns JSON, but there is no `outputSchema`/`structuredContent`/zod validation anywhere (`inputSchema` exists, nothing on the output side) — agents must trust the shape rather than check it. *Recommendation:* add an `outputSchema` alongside `inputSchema` in `apps/mcp-superserver/src/registry.ts`/`runtime.ts` and validate each tool's return value against it before wrapping.

10. **Per-task progress is silent by default on long unattended runs (Runtime #44, PARTIAL).** `logTask`/`logCacheHit`/`logDryRun` in `open-source/a0/src/runner.ts` (~979-991) are gated behind `--verbose`; a long unattended multi-lane run produces no default progress output — the exact silent-failure mode observability is meant to prevent. *Recommendation:* make a lightweight default (non-verbose) progress line print per lane/task/status, and if agent-to-agent (Task-tool) fanout needs the same, wire it into `control-plane.ts`'s event log.

11. **A real severity/alerting module is dead code (Operator #26, PARTIAL).** `apps/edge-workers/src/security/alerts.ts` defines severity levels and Discord-embed routing, but `grep -rln 'security/alerts'` across the live tree returns zero importers — it's never wired into a live request path. *Recommendation:* wire `detectSuspiciousActivity`/`sendAlert` into the real request-handling path, or delete it; make the deploy event-bus's `eventSeverity()` actually drive channel routing instead of only internal subscription filtering.

12. **Growing local journals have no retention policy (Operator #30, PARTIAL).** `.a0/control-plane/events.jsonl` (24MB and growing) and moonshine's `agent-runs/*.jsonl` have no rotation/prune logic, are gitignored, and CLAUDE.md itself calls them disposable — meaning they persist only by accident on one machine. *Recommendation:* define and implement an explicit retention window (e.g., ship to R2/D1 with a 90-day retention) instead of relying on incidental non-deletion.

13. **Retryable vs. logical-failure classification is conflated (Runtime #47, PARTIAL).** `agentic-chat.ts`'s `isRetryableToolError()` (~line 1740) bundles `'invalid'` (a logical/argument problem) into the same retryable bucket as `'timeout'`/`'rate'` (transient/terminal problems), even though `apps/edge-workers/src/lib/multi-arch/errors/taxonomy.ts` already has a clean `ErrorCategory`/`RecoveryAction` split. *Recommendation:* split into `transportError` vs `logicalError`, reusing the existing taxonomy, and route logical errors to `refineToolArgsAfterValidation` instead of a blind retry.

14. **No general summary/verbose toggle across the MCP tool surface (Agent #35, PARTIAL).** The failures-only default exists in the `a0 run` CLI (`cli.ts:1196`) and one hard-coded truncation exists in `registry.ts` (~712-810), but no `verbose`/`detail` input param convention exists across MCP tool schemas. *Recommendation:* add a shared `detail`/`verbose` param to the tool-definition generator so large-payload tools default to summarized output with an opt-in to full detail.

15. **No per-call model/reasoning-effort/cost telemetry (Runtime #45, MISSING).** `agentic-chat.ts` tracks `modelUsed` inline (lines 543, 796, 898, 910) but doesn't persist it; aggregate cost tracking (`apps/cli/src/admin/commands/costs.ts`) has no per-call granularity. *Recommendation:* emit a per-call telemetry record (model, reasoning-effort/params sent, tokens, latency) at the point `modelUsed` is set, into a structured log — pairs naturally with item 2.

16. **No task-context/intent disclosure param for ambiguous tool calls (Tool #61, MISSING).** No `taskContext`/`reason`/`intent` field exists anywhere in `apps/mcp-superserver/src` tool schemas. *Recommendation:* add an optional `taskContext`/`callReason` string to ambiguous tools (e.g. `adam-agent.ts` selection tools) and thread it into scoring/selection logic.

17. **No proactive quota-check primitive before a batch (Tool #62, PARTIAL).** Only `open-source/a0/src/seo.ts` (~497-520) engineers around Search Console's rate limit, via reactive pacing/backoff and idempotent-skip — not a queryable "remaining budget" the agent can check up front, and it's local to one script. *Recommendation:* extract the pacing/backoff pattern into a shared quota-aware batch helper that reports remaining budget before a batch starts.

18. **No poll-interval/backoff contract or webhook alternative (Tool #63, MISSING).** `todo_poll_google_signin` (`aic-tools.generated.ts` ~10040) is designed to be polled with no documented minimum interval or backoff schedule. *Recommendation:* document an explicit poll-interval/backoff contract on the tool description, consider a webhook/callback alternative, and add a repo-wide "avoid tight-loop polling" norm to AGENTS.md.

19. **No automated reconciliation of an agent's narrated summary against its actual tool-call ledger (Runtime #42, PARTIAL).** Moonshine's `.moonshine/agent-runs/*.jsonl` already separates `tool_request`/`tool_result` (ground truth) from `assistant_delta`/`final` (self-report), but nothing diffs them; the only built-in `agent verify` command is scoped to Lean proof verification. *Recommendation:* add `moonshine agent audit --run-id <id>` that diffs `final`/`assistant_delta` claims against `tool_result` events and flags unsupported claims.

20. **Replay and resume don't compose into "resume skipping known-good steps" (Runtime #46, PARTIAL).** `agent_runtime.rs`'s `agent replay` only reads and reprints the ledger; `agent resume` only continues past a human-approval gate; neither replays completed steps as pre-satisfied context. *Recommendation:* extend `agent resume` with a `--from-journal <run_id>` mode that replays completed events as context and only re-executes steps after the first failure.

21. **No operator-tunable alert thresholds or snooze mechanism (Operator #32, MISSING).** Depends on item 4 existing first — `checkWranglerCostGuardrails` limits are fixed/code-defined today, with no snooze/mute concept found anywhere in alerting-adjacent code. *Recommendation:* once the weekly digest job (item 4) exists, add a small per-alert-type threshold config plus a snooze-until timestamp checked before firing.

### 9. Composability, Orchestration & Ecosystem Fit

## Overall Read

Composability infrastructure in this repo is real but half-wired. The MCP superserver has genuine building blocks for a healthy tool ecosystem — a shared `ToolArtifactRole`/`consumes`/`produces` contract (`apps/mcp-superserver/src/types.ts`), a `.gg` FORK/FOLD topology DSL (`apps/mcp-superserver/src/workflows.ts`), batch endpoints for at least two tool families, and a domain-MCP-per-concern architecture (~170 small servers, not one mega-tool). But the pattern across nearly every PARTIAL finding is the same: **the primitive exists, and the thing that would make it actually load-bearing does not**. The composite-tool builder computes role-matching interactions but still makes the caller hand-supply every constituent's raw arguments (Agent #60); the workflow DSL declares FORK/FOLD but `advanceWorkflowRun` executes steps in a naive sequential loop that ignores `dependsOn` entirely (Agent #59); a cancel method exists in `ForgeWorkflowManager` but no MCP tool or CLI command reaches it (Runtime #6); a `Tracer` with `parentId` is wired into the agent-tick path but no call site ever populates `parentId` (Runtime #1). Separately, the actual agent-spawning code path used in production (`fireCannonLanes` in `open-source/a0/src/repair.ts`) has no concurrency cap and no total-spawn ceiling at all — a real, unaddressed resource/safety gap, not just an unfinished nicety. Net effect: an agent operating this ecosystem today gets genuine leverage from the tool contracts and small-tool architecture, but hits manual glue, silent polling, unreachable cancel paths, and unbounded fan-out the moment it tries to actually orchestrate rather than just call individual tools.

## Verdict Tally

| Verdict | Count |
|---|---|
| MET | 4 |
| PARTIAL | 14 |
| MISSING | 4 |
| UNCLEAR | 0 |
| **Total** | **22** |

MET: Agent #32 (batch endpoints), Tool #49 (produces/consumes contract), Agent #57 (small-domain-tool architecture), Runtime #4 (uniform BaseAgent interface, no dispatch switch).

## Prioritized Punch List

**1. No cap on concurrent or total spawned agent subprocesses (Runtime #2, MISSING + Runtime #8, MISSING)**
`fireCannonLanes` (`open-source/a0/src/repair.ts` ~lines 147-209) spawns one child process per repair lane unconditionally via `spawn(process.execPath, [gnodePath, 'x','tsx',...])` and `Promise.all(childProcesses)` — no `concurrency` semaphore and no total-spawn ceiling. A generic capped fork primitive already exists (`forkRaceFold({..., concurrency})` in `open-source/gnosis/distributed-inference-host/src/frf.ts`, tested) but isn't wired in.
*Recommendation:* Thread `forkRaceFold`'s `concurrency` param through `fireCannonLanes`, and add a `--max-agents=<n>` hard ceiling to `runner.ts` that rejects further lane spawns once the run's total agent-process count hits budget. This is the single highest-leverage fix in the area — it's a live safety gap in the actual production spawn path, not a documentation gap.

**2. No way to cancel a running workflow through any agent/user-facing surface (Runtime #6, PARTIAL)**
`ForgeWorkflowManager.cancelRun(runId)` exists (`open-source/aeon-workflow/src/manager.ts`) and is exported, but the MCP superserver's dispatch switch in `apps/mcp-superserver/src/runtime.ts` implements `workflow_get/validate/create/run/history/attach/perpetual_coordination` — no `workflow_cancel` case — and `open-source/a0/src/cli.ts` has no `cancel` command either.
*Recommendation:* Add a `network/workflow_cancel` MCP case calling the existing `cancelRun`, plus an `a0 workflow cancel <runId>` CLI command. Paired with item 1, this closes the "can't cap it and can't stop it" gap in the same subsystem.

**3. Declarative FORK/FOLD topology exists but isn't actually executed (Agent #59, PARTIAL)**
`apps/mcp-superserver/src/workflows.ts` defines a real `.gg` DSL with FORK/FOLD relations and `dependsOn` arrays (e.g. `SOVEREIGN_AUTONOMY_TOPOLOGY`), but the only execution path wired to it, `advanceWorkflowRun` (invoked via MCP tool `network/workflow_run`), is a `for (const step of workflow.steps)` loop marking every step running→success in array order — it ignores `dependsOn` and FORK/FOLD structure, doing no real parallelism or barrier-join. Meanwhile CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md's "fork/race/fold scheduler" guidance to agents is prose, not a callable primitive.
*Recommendation:* Wire `advanceWorkflowRun` to actually execute FORK branches concurrently and FOLD as a real join using the existing `open-source/a0/src/wallington.ts` fork/race primitives, making `network/workflow_run` real rather than order-preserving.

**4. Composite tools don't auto-wire outputs into the next tool's inputs (Agent #60, PARTIAL)**
`buildInteraction()`/`acceptComposite()` in `apps/mcp-superserver/src/closure.ts` compute role-matched interactions, but `buildCompositeInputSchema()` (~line 332) just nests each constituent tool's full original `inputSchema` under `inputs` — the caller still must supply every constituent's arguments by hand. The same gap recurs one layer up in `workflows.ts`'s `normalizeRunMetadata()`/`appendMetadataEnv()`, which hand-map fields between pipeline stages.
*Recommendation:* Extend `buildCompositeInputSchema` to detect literal produces/consumes field-name overlap and auto-project matching output keys into the next tool's input at execution time, replacing the hand-written mappers. This is the finding that would make the already-MET `ToolArtifactRole` contract (Tool #49) actually pay off operationally.

**5. Pagination convention diverges across sibling tools, one has no way to page at all (Tool #50, MISSING)**
`dash_studio_browse_table` uses `offset`, `dash_studio_browse_kv_keys` uses `cursor`, and `dash_studio_browse_r2_objects` has neither — just `limit`/`prefix` with no way to page past page one.
*Recommendation:* Standardize all `dash_studio_browse_*` (and other paginated) tools on cursor-based paging, and give R2 browsing an actual cursor param.

**6. No sandbox/dry-run routing for MCP tools that mutate state (Tool #54, MISSING)**
Zero hits grepping `apps/mcp-superserver/src/*.ts` and every `apps/*-mcp/src/tools.ts` for `dryRun`/`sandbox`/`staging`/test-mode routing. The a0 CLI's `--dry-run` flags only preview local destructive ops (cache prune, docker build); nothing routes an agent's test invocation of an MCP tool away from production.
*Recommendation:* Add a documented `MCP_SANDBOX=1` / per-call `dryRun` convention honored by state-mutating domain-MCP handlers, routing to a preview D1/KV binding or fixture instead of production; document in `apps/mcp-superserver/LOCAL_AGENTS.md`.

**7. No shared error-shape contract across the 505-tool generated surface (Tool #52, PARTIAL)**
Only the JSON-RPC transport layer has a shared constructor (`mcpError(id, code, message)`, `apps/mcp-superserver/src/runtime.ts` ~line 280) and a generic `isError` check for composite-tool success. The hundreds of individual backend tools in `aic-tools.generated.ts` have no verified common error-body shape beyond that.
*Recommendation:* Define one `ToolErrorEnvelope` type (`code, message, retryable, details`) in `types.ts` and add a smoke test (alongside `publication-contract.smoke.ts`) asserting every registered tool's error path serializes to it before registry admission.

**8. Remote-batch waiting is fixed-interval polling, not push (Runtime #5, PARTIAL)**
`waitForRemoteBatch` (`open-source/a0/src/runner.ts` ~lines 1146-1215) is a `while (pending.size > 0)` loop calling `sleep(REMOTE_TASK_POLL_MS)` (250ms) each iteration, despite a relay/websocket event layer (`onRelayEvent` in `open-source/a0/src/control-doc.ts`) already existing in the codebase.
*Recommendation:* Wire `waitForRemoteBatch`'s remote-claim wait onto the existing `onRelayEvent` handler instead of the sleep loop.

**9. Sub-agent fan-out is untraceable to its parent (Runtime #1, PARTIAL)**
`Tracer`/`Span{parentId}` is wired into `shared-utils/src/agent-runtime/engine.ts` for top-level ticks, but no call site anywhere passes `parentId` when starting a span, and the actual fan-out primitives (`ParallelAgent` in `orchestration/parallel.ts`, `GraphAgent`) never call `tracer.startSpan` at all.
*Recommendation:* Thread `parentSpanId`/`causingTaskId` through `RunConfig`/`RunResult` and have `ParallelAgent`/`SequentialAgent`/`GraphAgent` call `startSpan({parentId: ctx.parentSpanId})` per sub-agent run.

**10. Idle/stale watchdog is scoped to one workflow and is pull-only (Runtime #3, PARTIAL)**
`getPerpetualMachineCoordination` (`apps/mcp-superserver/src/workflows.ts`) computes active/stale machine status from heartbeat logs and is exposed as `network/workflow_perpetual_coordination`, but only for that one autonomy-repair workflow, and only on-demand query — no generic watchdog for arbitrary spawned sub-agents, and no proactive push.
*Recommendation:* Generalize the stale/active heuristic into a library any spawn site can register a heartbeat against, and have the superserver push a notification (the `PushNotification` tool already exists in this environment) when a registered agent crosses the threshold.

**11. Batch endpoints exist for only 2 of 505 tools (Tool #56, PARTIAL)**
Only `llm_batch_inference` and `fact_synthesize_batch` accept arrays; 503/505 tools have no batch sibling, so an agent handling N items still pays N round trips almost everywhere.
*Recommendation:* Auto-generate a `*_batch` sibling for any tool family supporting independent multi-item operations (D1 writes, KV puts, notification sends), following the `llm_batch_inference` precedent.

**12. Sovereign-tooling enforcement exists for react/three but not for the CLI-level mandate (Tool #53, PARTIAL)**
`.husky/pre-commit`'s "NO NEW RAW REACT/THREE GATE" is real and tested, but CLAUDE.md's parallel "SOVEREIGN TOOLING IS MANDATORY" claims (`a0 xr deploy` not `wrangler deploy`, `a0 gn status` not `git status`, `a0 flow` not raw git) have no equivalent hook anywhere — no PreToolUse hook in `.claude/settings.json`, no pre-commit check, no adoption telemetry.
*Recommendation:* Extend the `.husky/pre-commit` gate pattern (or a Claude Code PreToolUse hook) to flag raw `wrangler deploy`/`git commit`/`git push`/`pnpm install`, and add a lightweight JSONL adoption counter for `a0`/`gn`/`monster` usage. Given how emphatically CLAUDE.md itself states this policy as "task failure with no exceptions," the enforcement gap is more consequential than its PARTIAL label suggests.

**13. No general-purpose sub-agent handoff schema (Agent #58, PARTIAL)**
Only a narrow theorem-work convention (AGENTS.md B34a, "Next exploration") plus ad hoc post-hoc `docs/*_HANDOFF.md` files exist. B33a's Task-tool mandate tracks work but doesn't require a context/why field in the spawn brief.
*Recommendation:* Generalize B34a into a repo-wide handoff template (`Context/Why` + `Constraints` + `Next exploration`) required in any TaskCreate description or Agent-spawn prompt.

**14. Discovery surfaces exist but agents aren't told to use them first (Tool #55, PARTIAL)**
`/directory`, `/docs`, `/search`, `/matrix.json` are documented as unmetered and `network/search` is a real tool, but no AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md rule tells an agent to check them before invoking a heavier tool, and no generated tool description carries a "prefer lighter tool" hint.
*Recommendation:* Add an explicit AGENTS.md rule to call `network/search` before a domain-specific analysis tool, and bake "prefer-lighter-tool" hints into heavyweight tool descriptions.

**15. Field-naming consistency is incidental, not enforced (Agent #37, PARTIAL, overlapping Tool #51, PARTIAL)**
`<noun>Id` dominates the generated surface but nothing lints for it, and `dash_studio_browse_*` diverges on pagination (Tool #50) as a concrete symptom. Separately, `apps/gnosis-engine-mcp`/`apps/quorum-mcp` use `key`, `apps/coarsening-mcp` uses `name`, where the rest of the ecosystem uses `id`.
*Recommendation:* Add a schema-consistency check to `generate-aic-tools.ts` (or standalone CI script) scanning for canonical field names and failing the build on divergence; rename the `key`/`name` outliers to match the `id` convention.

**16. Model substitution isn't logged/warned, and no "effort" override exists (Runtime #7, PARTIAL)**
`RunConfig.model` override and `modelSubstituted` response metadata both exist, but nothing logs a warning when substitution happens, and there's no `effort`/`reasoningEffort` concept anywhere in the edgework-sdk agent tree.
*Recommendation:* Add a `logger.warn` gated on `modelSubstituted` where `eadk-provider.ts` consumes `_meta`, and add an explicit `effort` field to `RunConfig` alongside `model`.

### 10. Lifecycle, Versioning & Change Management

## Overall read

Lifecycle and change management exists only at the coarse, whole-server level today: a single `SERVER_VERSION` and `MCP_PROTOCOL_VERSION` constant in `apps/mcp-superserver/src/runtime.ts`, a rollback-capable deploy history in `open-source/x-ranger/src/state.ts`, and Nx-level build-cache resumability under `.a0/cache`. Below that layer, the ~190-tool domain-MCP surface has no per-tool contract version, no deprecation flag, no call-level usage telemetry, and no migration path — the one clean win is that `apps/mcp-server`'s tool catalog is generated (not hand-duplicated) from a single authored registry. In practice this means an agent (or engineer) has no machine-readable way to know a tool's contract changed, no way to see which of ~190 tools are ever actually called, and no safety net for a bad deploy landing mid-task. This is an early-stage area: the deploy/rollback plumbing is real, but tool-level versioning, deprecation, telemetry, and migration are essentially greenfield.

## Verdict tally

- **MET**: 1 (Tool #45 — generated tool catalog derives from one authored registry)
- **PARTIAL**: 7 (Runtime #58, Runtime #24, Runtime #57, Tool #41, Tool #42, Tool #47, Tool #48)
- **MISSING**: 10 (Agent #61, Tool #43, Tool #44, Tool #46, Runtime #23, Runtime #59, Runtime #60, Runtime #61, Runtime #62, Runtime #64)
- **UNCLEAR**: 0

## Prioritized punch list

1. **No per-tool contract version at all** (Agent #61, Tool #41 — MISSING/PARTIAL). Neither the shared `Tool` type in `open-source/mcp-framework/src/types.ts` nor `NetworkTool`/`CompositeToolSpec` in `apps/mcp-superserver/src/types.ts` carries a version field — only the JSON-RPC handshake constant `MCP_PROTOCOL_VERSION = '2024-11-05'` exists, and that's protocol-level, not per-tool. This is the root blocker for nearly everything else below (deprecation, run pinning, migration). *Fix*: add `contractVersion`/`schemaVersion` to the shared `Tool` type and to `NetworkTool`/`CompositeToolSpec`, populate it in each `<domain>-mcp/src/tools.ts` and in `generate-aic-tools.ts`, and surface it in `tools/list` (`runtime.ts` `serializeTool`) so agents can detect a contract bump.

2. **No deprecation/removal lifecycle for the ~190-tool domain-MCP surface** (Tool #43 — MISSING). Tools are wired via hand-written static imports in `apps/mcp-superserver/src/registry.ts`; removing one requires manually finding every import/registration line, and `deprecated` returns zero grep hits anywhere under `apps/mcp-superserver/src` or the domain `-mcp` packages. *Fix*: add a `deprecated: true`/`removedAt` field to tool metadata and a CI check in the existing `generate:*` pipeline that fails the build if a tool deprecated for N days is still exported.

3. **No call-level telemetry — can't tell spec'd-but-never-called from actually-invoked** (Tool #46, Runtime #23, Tool #44 — all MISSING). No `invocationCount`/`callCount`/`usageStats` anywhere in `apps/mcp-superserver/src` or `open-source/gnosis`; `.a0/control-plane/events.jsonl` only logs Nx build/typecheck task events, and `ledger-tool-audit.generated.json` tracks proof-coverage, not runtime calls — so there's no way to see which tools/parameters go unused across sessions for pruning. *Fix*: add call-logging middleware in `runtime.ts`'s `tools/call` dispatch path that appends `{tool, timestamp, argsShape, paramKeys}` to a JSONL log, then a periodic `a0` report (e.g. `tools:usage-report`) diffing that log against `registry.ts`'s full catalog to flag zero-call tools and always-empty parameters.

4. **No per-run pinning of tool/prompt versions** (Runtime #61 — MISSING). Only the single hardcoded `MCP_PROTOCOL_VERSION` exists; zero hits for `runManifest`/`pinnedVersion`/`lockVersion`/`versionLock` across `open-source/a0/src` and `apps/mcp-superserver/src`. Directly blocked by item 1. *Fix*: once tool contract versions exist, stamp each session/workflow run with a manifest (e.g. `.a0/runs/<runId>/manifest.json`) capturing resolved tool-schema versions and prompt hash at task start, and have the dispatch path refuse/warn on a mid-run deploy that changes a pinned tool's version.

5. **Workflow run state doesn't survive isolate restart** (Runtime #58 — PARTIAL). Build/verify task state genuinely resumes via the disk-backed `CorridorCache` in `open-source/a0/src/runner.ts` (`.a0/cache`, `a0.task.cache_hit` events), but `apps/mcp-superserver/src/workflows.ts`'s `ForgeWorkflowManager`/`QDoc` is a module-level singleton (`let workflowDoc: QDoc | null = null`) with no `durable_objects`/`kv_namespaces`/`d1_databases` binding in `apps/mcp-superserver/aeon.toml` — an in-flight agent workflow is lost on Worker eviction even though the QDoc format itself supports snapshotting (`qdoc.canonical-snapshot.v1` in `open-source/gnosis/src/crdt/qdoc.ts`). *Fix*: wire periodic `toCanonicalSnapshot()`/`applyCanonicalSnapshot()` calls to a Durable Object or D1 binding added to `aeon.toml`.

6. **Caller-error vs execution-error collapse to one JSON-RPC code** (Tool #47 — PARTIAL). `runtime.ts` distinguishes -32602 (bad tool name) from -32603 (everything else), but once a valid tool is invoked, any thrown error — caller misuse or genuine bug — collapses to -32603, polluting any future reliability metrics. *Fix*: introduce typed `ToolInputError` vs `ToolExecutionError`, map the former to -32602 and the latter to -32603 in the `tools/call` catch block, and log the split.

7. **Atomic full-replace deploys with no in-flight version safety** (Runtime #24 — PARTIAL). `open-source/x-ranger/src/state.ts` gives real post-hoc rollback (`XRangerVersionRecord`), but `apps/mcp-superserver/aeon.toml` has no gradual-deployment/traffic-split config, so an agent mid `tools/call` when a new version deploys can be served new code with zero draining protection. *Fix*: enable Cloudflare Workers gradual deployments for the mcp-superserver Worker via `a0 xr deploy`, and/or echo the session's starting `SERVER_VERSION` so a session can detect and refuse to continue against a differing mid-task version.

8. **No migration/breaking-change process for tool contract changes** (Runtime #62 — MISSING). Zero hits for "migration guide," "breaking change," or `deprecated` across `apps/mcp-superserver` and `open-source/gnosis`; no adapter/alias layer for renamed/reshaped parameters, no documented process in AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/README. Depends on item 1's `schemaVersion`. *Fix*: pair `schemaVersion` with an explicit `MIGRATIONS.md` or `migratesFrom` field documenting old→new parameter mappings per `canonicalName`, and have `runtime.ts` accept both shapes for at least one deploy cycle.

9. **No changelog for the fast-moving runtime layers** (Runtime #64 — MISSING). Only the root `CHANGELOG.md` (stale since 2026-06-10, "Unreleased" empty, describes January product features) and `packages/edgework-node/CHANGELOG.md` exist — nothing for `open-source/gnosis`, `open-source/a0`, `apps/mcp-superserver`, or `apps/mcp-server`, despite daily commits like "rebuild faceswap WASM ... + bump gnosis." *Fix*: add a `CHANGELOG.md` to `open-source/gnosis` (and/or `apps/mcp-superserver`) and wire an `a0 flow`/`gn flow` post-commit step that appends an entry keyed to the bumped version.

10. **No heartbeat fallback for cron-driven loops** (Runtime #59 — MISSING). `scripts/a0-flow-cron.sh` relies solely on an externally-installed crontab entry with only an overlap-prevention lock file; `.a0/mcp-app-submission-loop.log` shows a single sleep-and-rewake loop; no secondary wake signal exists for either. *Fix*: add a long-period fallback check (a second, longer crontab entry, or a startup check comparing last-run timestamp against expected cadence) that re-fires the loop if the primary signal hasn't landed within N× its interval.

11. **No determinism guard for workflow/topology scripts** (Runtime #60 — MISSING). `eslint.config.js` explicitly sets `'no-restricted-syntax': 'off'` rather than banning `Date.now()`/`Math.random()` in workflow code; nothing in `open-source/aeon-workflow`, `apps/mcp-superserver`, or the `.gg` topology tooling restricts non-deterministic calls needed for safe replay. *Fix*: add a scoped eslint rule banning direct `Date.now()`/`Math.random()`/raw crypto-randomness calls outside an injected clock/rng context in workflow/topology directories.

12. **Tool docs and input schemas are dual-authored with no drift check** (Tool #48 — PARTIAL). Example: `apps/mcp-server/src/tools/behaviors.ts` has a zod `BehaviorsListSchema` with `.describe()` calls and a separately hand-written raw JSON Schema `inputSchema` with independent description strings (lines ~187-213) — no test cross-checks them. *Fix*: derive `inputSchema` from the zod schema via `zod-to-json-schema`, or add a registry test asserting documented parameter names still exist in `inputSchema.properties`.

13. **Workflow re-trigger always restarts from scratch** (Runtime #57 — PARTIAL). `open-source/aeon-workflow/src/manager.ts`'s `triggerWorkflow()` sets every step to `status: 'pending'` on each trigger with no resume-from-prior-run path, even though Nx-level build caching (`nx.json`, `.a0/cache`) proves the pattern works elsewhere. *Fix*: add a `resumeFromRunId` path to `ForgeWorkflowManager.triggerWorkflow` that copies prior `success` step results and only re-queues steps whose upstream inputs changed.

14. **No documented/tested protocol compatibility matrix** (Tool #42 — PARTIAL). The deploy topology (separate Worker for `apps/mcp-superserver`, stdio bridge via `.mcp.json`) already decouples tool-server releases from harness releases, but `MCP_PROTOCOL_VERSION` is a single hardcoded literal with no negotiation matrix, documented compatibility range, or test proving an older/newer harness still works. *Fix*: negotiate/advertise a supported `protocolVersion` range in the `initialize` response and add a `runtime.test.ts` case calling the worker with an older `protocolVersion` to prove backward compatibility.

### 11. Human Control, Communication & Collaboration

## Overall Read

Human-control and collaboration norms in this repo are unusually well-*articulated* for the things Forkjoin's own incidents have already burned it on — sovereign tooling (B-series in AGENTS.md), scope discipline (B19, B7), destructive-action confirmation (B5), and durable cross-session memory (the auto-injected MEMORY.md index, ~140+ entries) are all MET and genuinely load-bearing. But the area has a structural self-contradiction running through it: AGENTS.md's "Agent Operational Guardrails" ("Never ask for user input — continue autonomously until 100% complete," "Never stop until complete") is written as an unconditional default, and it directly undercuts a cluster of otherwise-reasonable human-control stories (interrupt-and-resume, stop-on-command, ask-when-truly-blocked, plan-before-acting). That one guardrail is the single biggest lever in this area — fixing it resolves three MISSING findings at once. Beyond that, most of the gaps are the same shape repeated: a real mechanism exists (dry-run, Task tools, permission allowlists, RemoteTrigger/Cron, ReportFindings CONFIRMED/PLAUSIBLE) but it's scoped narrowly to one code path or one domain rather than generalized into a repo-wide convention, and enforcement is almost entirely prose-based rather than tool- or hook-backed.

| Verdict | Count |
|---|---|
| MET | 7 |
| PARTIAL | 16 |
| MISSING | 9 |
| **Total** | **32** |

MET: User #22, #51, #52, #24, #15, #49, #54
PARTIAL: User #1, #2, #3, #4, #6, #8, #9, #11, #12, #13, #16, #17, #21, #50, #53, #55

## Prioritized Punch List

**1. Reconcile the "never stop until complete" guardrail with legitimate stop/ask conditions — resolves 3 MISSING findings at once (User #14, #20, #19)**
AGENTS.md lines 566–570 ("Never ask for user input — continue autonomously until 100% complete, except on confirmed hallucination," "Never stop until complete — pause only for long-running processes or hallucination confirmation") is a direct policy conflict with: a human saying "stop" mid-task (#14), a genuine human-judgment decision the agent can't infer from repo context (#20), and pre-implementation Plan Mode for nontrivial work (#19, `grep -in '\bplan\b' AGENTS.md` returns zero hits). Fix: add explicit carve-outs alongside the hallucination exception — an unambiguous human stop phrase halts immediately; a decision requiring human judgment (ambiguous product/business tradeoff, irreversible-but-not-hallucination choice) pauses and asks; nontrivial multi-file changes get a written/Plan-Mode checkpoint before edits begin. Then run `pnpm run a0 -- agentfile sync` to propagate into CLAUDE.md.

**2. Remove the blanket `"Bash"` allowlist entry (User #10)**
`.claude/settings.local.json`'s `permissions.allow` array contains a bare `"Bash"` entry alongside dozens of scoped `Bash(specific-command)` patterns — this pre-approves every Bash invocation, including `rm -rf`, `git rm`, force-kills, contradicting AGENTS.md B5 ("Ask about destruction") which is otherwise MET at the policy-text level. Fix: replace with scoped `Bash(<command> *)` patterns as the rest of the file already does, and add a PreToolUse hook that pattern-matches destructive commands (`rm -rf`, `git push --force`, `kill -9`, `wrangler`/`gcloud` mutations) to force confirmation regardless of allowlist state. Pairs naturally with User #12's broader ask to add a `deny`/`ask` tier.

**3. Announce scope expansion before acting on it (User #7 — MISSING)**
B19 ("Fixing bug X while noticing Y: file Y, don't merge them") tells the agent not to *do* unrelated work but never requires it to *say* when it's about to go beyond the literal ask, and the "never ask for user input" guardrail actively discourages any such pause. Fix: add an explicit norm — before touching unrelated files, adding dependencies, or expanding a fix into a refactor, state the expansion and why.

**4. Detect conflicts between new requests and prior recorded decisions (User #56 — MISSING)**
MEMORY.md persists ~140+ prior decisions and is auto-injected every session, but nothing instructs the agent to diff an incoming request against it. Fix: add an AGENTS.md norm — before executing a request touching a topic already covered in MEMORY.md or a repo decision doc, diff against the recorded decision and flag conflicts instead of silently complying or silently overriding.

**5. Generalize verified-vs-assumed tagging beyond code review (User #2, #4 — both PARTIAL, same gap)**
The `ReportFindings` tool's `CONFIRMED`/`PLAUSIBLE` verdict enum is the one place this exists, and it's scoped to code review only. AGENTS.md's "Vibe-Science"/"When the system is uncertain, say so explicitly" is prose-only and unscoped elsewhere; the screenshot ("say so, do not guess") and hallucination-stop-work carve-outs are narrow single-purpose instances of the same underlying need. Fix: promote a lightweight `Verified:` / `Assuming:` inline convention into AGENTS.md's general Expectations section, folding the three narrow carve-outs into one general rule.

**6. Add provenance/citation convention for codebase claims vs. general knowledge (User #5 — MISSING)**
No mechanism distinguishes "this came from your codebase" from "this is general model knowledge" — grepped for provenance/citation/grounded/attribution, no hits outside unrelated matches. Fix: require inline path citations (`path/to/file.ts:42`) for claims about repo behavior, and a `[general knowledge]` marker otherwise, enforced the way B2/B4 enforce other Intellectual Honesty rules.

**7. Extend dry-run/preview from a0's deploy path into MCP-exposed mutating tools (User #1)**
`open-source/a0/src/executor.ts` (`--dry-run` for `a0 xr deploy`), `control-plane.ts` (`dry_run` task status), and `void-walk.ts` (`dryRun` preview mode) all prove the pattern, but `apps/mcp-superserver/src` tool definitions (`aic-tools.generated.ts`, `skymesh-tools.ts`, `forgo-trd-tools.ts`) have no `dryRun`/`preview` param on any mutating tool. Fix: add a `preview`/`dryRun` boolean to mutating MCP tool schemas and mandate it in AGENTS.md's Tooling Mandate for new tools.

**8. Attach a policy-id/reason to blocked actions (User #6)**
B20 ("Explain refusals... is not acceptable" without context) is a real written mandate, but `.claude/settings.json`/`settings.local.json` have no `deny` list and no hooks — refusal explanation is entirely self-applied prose with no rule ID the user could go edit. Fix: add a `deny` list (or PreToolUse hook) with a short reason string per pattern (e.g. deny `Bash(wrangler deploy*)` → "sovereign-tooling: use a0 xr deploy"). This is the natural companion fix to item #2 above.

**9. Add a proactive-pushback norm (User #8 — PARTIAL)**
Only three narrow cases of not silently complying exist (B5 destruction, B6a quality degradation, B20 refusal explanation); nothing tells the agent to flag a request it thinks is architecturally unsound outside those categories. Fix: extend the Intellectual Honesty section with a norm to flag concerns and propose alternatives before implementing anything likely to cause regression, security issues, or architectural harm not already covered by B5/B6a.

**10. Generalize the labeled-handoff pattern beyond formal/theorem work (User #55)**
B34a's "Next exploration" labeled handoff is real but scoped only to agent-to-agent theorem work; B6a and the hallucination stop-work rule cover two other narrow trigger conditions. There's no generic "done" or closing-summary convention for ordinary tasks. Fix: add a Standard Workflow step requiring any task ending blocked/partial/uncertain to close with a labeled "Next: <what only the user can decide>" section, for all task types.

**11. Make `a0 review`-style diff summaries a default close-out step, not invoke-it-yourself (User #3)**
`a0 review <ref>` (`open-source/a0/src/review.ts`), `a0 gn status --json`, and `a0 flow`'s embedded file/line stats all exist but none render unprompted at task end. Fix: add a convention (AGENTS.md + `verify` skill checklist) that agents run `a0 review <ref>` and paste its summary into the final response by default.

**12. Fix `a0 gn flow`'s auto-commit messages to follow the documented convention, and document branch/PR structure (User #50)**
Git log shows the tool's own generated commits ("gn flow: 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)") violating the `<type>(<scope>): <subject>` format AGENTS.md documents (line 516) and that other commits in this same repo follow correctly (e.g. "chore(distributed-inference-worker): rebuild faceswap WASM..."). Branch naming and PR title/body templates are undocumented entirely. Fix: derive type/scope from changed paths in the auto-commit generator; add a branch-naming convention and PR template to AGENTS.md.

**13. Gate cloud-routine prompts with the same sovereign-tooling check used interactively (User #16)**
Memory file `rogue-cloud-routines-disabled.md` documents a real incident: an unattended RemoteTrigger routine ran raw `wrangler deploy` (a documented violation) and reintroduced a paid cost regression, requiring manual hunt-and-disable since the API can't even delete routines. Fix: apply a0's sovereign-tooling check to RemoteTrigger-created prompts, and add an `a0` audit subcommand listing all `enabled:true` routines flagged for banned raw commands.

**14. Turn the B33 task-stack convention into an automated check (User #11)**
TaskUpdate's `addBlockedBy`/`addBlocks` support push-not-replace semantics per B33/B33a, but nothing verifies agents actually comply — a task could silently disappear rather than transition through `in_progress → completed`. Fix: add a lightweight CI/script check that inspects TaskList history per session and flags silent replacement.

**15. Add a status-update cadence rule for long-running sovereign commands (User #21)**
The `Monitor` tool and B33a's task surfacing help, but AGENTS.md has zero hits for "notify"/"checkpoint"/"progress"/"status update" — no policy dictates cadence for narrated status during a multi-minute `a0 run --all` or WASM rebuild. Fix: require one status line per meaningful phase boundary for long-running sovereign commands, in Monitor-friendly format.

**16. Add a "paused" task-lifecycle status for mid-task interruption (User #13)**
`TaskStop` only terminates; TaskCreate/TaskUpdate has pending/in_progress/completed/deleted but no paused state, so there's no pause-and-resume-without-losing-state for agent tasks (as distinct from ML inference checkpoint/resume, which is unrelated and already solved). Fix: add a "paused" status to the B33/B33a task lifecycle plus a convention for serializing enough context (TaskGet description + last-known diffs) for cold resume.

**17. Scope the remaining blanket tool allows and add a destructive-class ask tier (User #12)**
Beyond the bare `"Bash"` fix in item #2, `settings.local.json` also grants unscoped `Agent`, `Task`, `Edit`, `Write`, `Read`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `WebFetch`, `WebSearch`, `NotebookEdit`. Fix: scope these down the way the Bash subset already is, with a `deny`/`ask` tier for destructive classes.

**18. Scale response depth/length to question complexity (User #17)**
CLAUDE.md's "Be concise, concrete, and code-focused" is applied uniformly rather than scaling with the ask's size. Fix: add "size the response to the question — a quick fix gets a quick answer, a nontrivial change gets a fuller account."

**19. Add jargon/depth calibration and default plain-language-first summaries (User #18, #23 — both MISSING, same gap)**
No policy calibrates explanation depth to demonstrated user fluency (#18), and no default "plain summary first, expand on request" behavior exists for agent responses (#23) — the only adjacent artifact, `open-source/aeon-markdown`'s depth-lens/ELI5 renderer, is reader-facing for research documents, not wired into agent conversation. Fix: add a Style rule for depth calibration from context signals, and a default-verbosity rule leading with a jargon-free summary before drill-down detail — both build on item #18's fix above.

**20. Introduce an opt-in-heavy / default-light orchestration switch (User #53)**
B33a and B34 mandate TaskCreate and fork/race/fold unconditionally for every request "including single-task requests," the inverse of opt-in overhead. Fix: reserve mandatory task-stack scaffolding and fork/race/fold for requests the user marks complex or that cross a stated threshold, rather than mandating both for all requests. Lowest urgency of the list — this is a deliberate process-heaviness tradeoff rather than a safety or correctness gap, so treat it as a design decision to revisit rather than an urgent fix.

### 12. Platform Security Operations

## Platform Security Operations — Gap Coverage

### Overall read

Security in this repo is a story of real, well-built point solutions that don't add up to a coherent platform posture. The genuine engineering is substantial: a default-deny UCAN/capability engine (`open-source/auth`), a macOS sandbox-exec profile gate in `monster-guard/src/main.rs`, a network-closed-by-default `gnexec` runtime, 77 `wrangler.jsonc` + 184 D1 migrations + Terraform for the KMS/custodial-signer as real IaC, and a danger-tiered type-to-confirm deploy guard in `apps/cli/src/admin/safety/confirmation.ts`. But almost every one of these is scoped to a single subsystem rather than applied platform-wide, and the two places that most determine day-to-day blast radius — the Claude Code permission grants in `.claude/settings*.json` and the mandated `a0 xr deploy` path — have none of the discipline the repo already knows how to build elsewhere. Secret *detection* exists (`packages/polyglot-scanner-core` `detectHardcodedSecrets()`) but doesn't actually block anything (pre-commit skips it, pre-push swallows the failure). There is no centralized secret store, no key inventory, no rotation tooling, no backup/restore automation for D1/R2, and no break-glass runbook — despite a real incident on file (`rogue-cloud-routines-disabled.md`) where the operator improvised exactly this response live. Docs like `docs/SECURITY.md` are largely AWS/HIPAA-flavored boilerplate that doesn't match the actual Cloudflare/GCP/D1/gnit stack, while feature-specific runbooks (Broadcast resilience, Halos Agency privacy addendum) show the team can write the real thing when it's scoped narrowly — that discipline just hasn't been generalized to the platform level yet.

### Verdict tally

| PARTIAL | 21 |
| MISSING | 15 |
| UNCLEAR | 0 |
| **Total** | **40** |

MET: #10 (capability/sandbox least-privilege), #22 (network-closed-by-default), #35 (deploy IaC coverage), #54 (product-specific ToS/AUP pages).

### Prioritized punch list

**Tier 1 — live enforcement gaps (detection exists, blocking doesn't)**

1. **#11 (PARTIAL)** — `detectHardcodedSecrets()` in `packages/polyglot-scanner-core/src/analyzer.ts` is real but toothless: `.husky/pre-commit` never calls it, and `.husky/pre-push`'s `|| { echo ⚠️ ... }` swallows a positive finding instead of exiting 1. → Wire the scanner into pre-commit (staged-diff only, for speed) and make pre-push actually `exit 1` on a `HARDCODED_SECRET` hit.
2. **#21 (MISSING)** — `.claude/settings.local.json` grants blanket unscoped `Bash`/`Agent`/`Task`/`Edit`/`Write`/etc. with no capability/blast-radius documentation anywhere (`apps/mcp-superserver/README.md` has none). → Add a per-tool capability table to the MCP superserver docs and replace bare `"Bash"` with scoped patterns.
3. **#17 (PARTIAL)** — The `a0 xr deploy`-only convention is documented in AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md but not enforced: no `deny` rules in `.claude/settings.json` block raw `wrangler deploy` or prod-mutating `gcloud`. → Add explicit deny patterns forcing deploys through `a0 xr deploy`.
4. **#18 (PARTIAL)** — The real type-the-word + countdown confirmation system (`apps/cli/src/admin/safety/confirmation.ts`) never made it into the CLAUDE.md-mandated `open-source/x-ranger` path; `x-ranger/src/teardown.ts` (drops a live Worker) is gated by a bare `--confirm` boolean. → Port the confirmation pattern into x-ranger's teardown/prod-deploy paths.
5. **#13 (MISSING)** — No runtime secret-leak detection: no gitleaks/trufflehog/secretlint config, no Sentry scrubbing hook, no PostToolUse hook scanning tool output/logs for credential shapes. → Add a `.claude/settings.json` PostToolUse hook (or log-drain filter) regex-scanning for AWS/GCP/JWT/Cloudflare-token shapes, alerting via `PushNotification`.

**Tier 2 — identity, credentials, and revocation**

6. **#9 (PARTIAL)** — No centralized secret store: `scripts/upload-cloudflare-secrets.mjs` fans a local plaintext `.env` out to ~77 per-worker Cloudflare secret bindings; the `SecretsService` described in `docs/SECURITY.md` has zero implementation (`grep -rn "class SecretsService"` = 0 hits). → Stand up one real secret store (Cloudflare Secrets Store or gnosis-backed vault) as source of truth.
7. **#14 (MISSING)** — No key inventory: nothing aggregates which secrets exist, where they're used, or when last rotated. → Add `a0 keys audit`, deriving a table from `wrangler.jsonc` bindings + known env-var names, modeled on `scripts/verify-business-docs.ts`.
8. **#12 (MISSING)** — No rotation tooling/cadence anywhere; the only "rotation" code is an illustrative AWS/Postgres `SecretsService.rotateSecret()` snippet that doesn't match this stack. → Add `a0 secrets rotate <name>` plus a scheduled age-threshold reminder via `CronCreate`.
9. **#15 (PARTIAL)** — GCP and forgo-mirror auth have dedicated service-account/machine-key patterns, but `.a0/auth.json`'s GitHub origin-push credential has no documented separation from the operator's personal account. → Create/document a scoped bot GitHub identity for the origin-push leg.
10. **#16 (PARTIAL)** — `docs/FORGO_MIRROR_AUTH.md` gives the forgo leg a short-lived-token-or-scoped-PAT choice, but explicitly states GitHub push "uses your normal git/SSH credentials" — the most-used publish path is the least scoped. → Default `--github-writes` to a fine-grained, repo-scoped PAT.
11. **#19 (PARTIAL)** — Real revocation exists only in `open-source/zedge/companion` (`revokeGrant`/`revokeAudience`/`revokeMode`); `delegation_revoke` is declared in `apps/mcp-superserver/src/aic-tools.generated.ts` but has no handler; nothing revokes Claude Code sessions, MCP client connections, or Cloudflare/GitHub tokens in one action. → Build `a0 security revoke-all` fanning out to all these surfaces; wire the missing `delegation_revoke` handler.

15. **#20 (PARTIAL)** — Prod/staging secrets are logically separated via `wrangler secret put --env`, but share one Cloudflare account/API-token scope, and neither AGENTS.md nor CLAUDE.md states this as policy. → Document the existing per-env scoping as explicit policy; consider a separate staging API token/account.

### 13. Customization, Configuration & Guardrail Automation

## Overall read

Customization and configuration are genuinely solid: Claude Code's settings hierarchy (global `~/.claude/settings.json` → project `.claude/settings.json`/`settings.local.json`) is actively exercised with distinct, non-trivial content at each layer, a dedicated `update-config` skill exists for editing it, skills are a documented and already-used extension point (three project-scoped skills live under `.claude/skills/`), directory-scoped capability restriction works both for permission rules and for skills, and selective deactivation of autonomous routines has a real precedent (`rogue-cloud-routines-disabled.md`).

Guardrail *automation*, by contrast, is where the area is weakest. The building blocks are real and often well-engineered — `open-source/a0/src/cloudflare-cost.ts`, `open-source/a0/src/security.ts`, `open-source/a0/src/dependency-policy.ts`, `open-source/gnosis/src/mesh-policy-edge.ts`'s Lean-anchored default-deny — but they are systematically **not wired to the paths that are actually mandated or actually run**: the canonical `a0 xr deploy` has no guardrail calls at all, CI security/cost audits run with `continue-on-error: true` or aren't invoked in workflows at all, and the audit CLI itself fails *open* by default (`--strict` is opt-in) in direct contradiction to the fail-closed posture the repo already proved out in `mesh-policy-edge.ts`. Worst of all, the documented "standard workflow" in CONTRIBUTING.md tells contributors to skip hooks (`--no-verify`) as steps 6–7, institutionalizing exactly the guardrail-bypass the audit is checking for. So: strong config *infrastructure*, weak and inconsistently-enforced automated *guardrails* on top of it.

## Verdict tally

| Verdict | Count | Tags |
|---|---|---|
| MET | 5 | User #57, User #58, User #59, User #62, User #64 |
| PARTIAL | 8 | User #60, User #61, User #63, Operator #57, Operator #58, Operator #60, Operator #62, Operator #63 |
| MISSING | 3 | Operator #59, Operator #61, Operator #64 |
| UNCLEAR | 0 | — |
| **Total** | **16** | |

## Prioritized punch list

1. **Operator #64 — MISSING.** CONTRIBUTING.md (lines 354–358) prescribes `git commit --no-verify` and `git push --no-verify` as the *normal* steps 6–7 of the standard workflow, not an emergency escape hatch, and CLAUDE.md's "never skip hooks unless explicitly asked" has zero technical enforcement (no hook-skip detector, no wrapper stripping the flag). This is the root-cause item: every other guardrail below assumes hooks/checks actually run, and this makes bypass the documented default.
   *Recommendation:* Add a real technical guardrail — a CI/server-side check that fails any PR/push made with `--no-verify` when hooks would have run (e.g. missing hook-generated trailers/artifacts) — and rewrite CONTRIBUTING.md so `--no-verify` is an explicit, logged exception path, not the happy path.

2. **Operator #57 — PARTIAL.** Real automatic deploy-blocking exists in `open-source/a0/src/forge.ts` `runForgeDeploy()` (~lines 940–965) for critical cost/dependency/certification violations, but the CLAUDE.md-mandated deploy command, `a0 xr deploy` (`open-source/a0/src/xranger.ts`), has zero references to cost/budget/dependency-policy checks — it only rewrites `--cwd` and shells out.
   *Recommendation:* Wire `checkWranglerCostGuardrails` (and ideally `auditDependencyPolicy`) into `runXRanger()` as a pre-flight check, so the deploy path operators are actually told to use enforces the same gate that already exists in `forge.ts`.

3. **Operator #63 — PARTIAL.** `a0 audit cloudflare` (and sibling `a0 audit *` subcommands) only return a blocking exit code when the caller passes `--strict` (`open-source/a0/src/cli.ts`); absent that flag, critical violations print but the command exits 0 — fail-open, the inverse of the fail-closed discipline already proven in `open-source/gnosis/src/mesh-policy-edge.ts` (`emptyPortListSealsRegion`, zero-sorry Lean theorem).
   *Recommendation:* Flip the CLI default so critical-severity violations block by default, with an explicit `--permissive`/`--advisory` opt-out for genuinely non-blocking reports.

4. **Operator #58 — PARTIAL.** `a0 audit security` and `a0 audit cloudflare --strict` can gate on critical findings via exit code, but neither is invoked in any `.github/workflows/*.yml` (only `audit aeon-flux` and `audit github-actions` run in CI). The security scanning that *does* run, `scanner-gate.yml`, marks every scan step `continue-on-error: true`, so a failed scan uploads SARIF but never blocks merge.
   *Recommendation:* Wire `pnpm run a0 -- audit security` and `pnpm run a0 -- audit cloudflare --strict` into `pr-check.yml` (or a new required workflow) without `continue-on-error`, and remove `continue-on-error` from `scanner-gate.yml`'s scan steps.

5. **Operator #59 — MISSING.** No software-composition-analysis / dependency-vulnerability check exists anywhere: no `pnpm audit`/Dependabot/Renovate/Snyk config found in repo root, `package.json`, or `.github/workflows/*.yml`. `apps/polyglot-scanner`'s CVE corpus is a benchmark for its own SAST rules, not a check of installed package versions against a vuln DB.
   *Recommendation:* Add a sovereign-wrapped dependency-vulnerability check (`pnpm audit --audit-level=high --json` or an OSV.dev batch query against `pnpm-lock.yaml`) as `a0 audit dependencies`, matching `auditCloudflare`'s violations/severity shape, and add it as a required CI gate.

6. **Operator #61 — MISSING.** No "notify-then-wait" primitive exists anywhere for autonomous actions: `open-source/a0/src/autoscale.ts` has a `dryRun` flag but on a live tick spawns/drains workers immediately; `open-source/a0/src/progressive-deploy.ts` computes a staged canary/toe-in/half/promote plan with `waitMs`/`verifyHealth` per stage, but `cli.ts` only *prints* the plan (`printProgressiveDeployPlan`) before `runForgeDeploy` proceeds — nothing actually pauses or blocks on human ack.
   *Recommendation:* Make progressive-deploy stages in `open-source/a0/src/forge.ts` actually enforce `waitMs`/`verifyHealth` between promotions, and add a notification step (SendMessage/PushNotification tooling is already available) plus a default delay window before any non-dry-run autoscale or auto-remediation action fires.

7. **Operator #60 — PARTIAL.** `required-buley-approval.yml` requires human review on every non-draft PR and infra files (e.g. `wrangler.toml`) are visible in normal PR diffs, but there's no purpose-built infra-change summary: `wrangler deploy --dry-run` output (`open-source/x-ranger/src/cli.ts` ~line 2264) isn't summarized, and `deploy-forgocd.yml` has no plan-review gate distinct from general code review.
   *Recommendation:* Add a CI step on `wrangler.toml`/`aeon.toml` changes that runs `a0 audit cloudflare --json` before/after and posts the cost/binding delta as a PR comment via `github-script`.

8. **Operator #62 — PARTIAL.** Cost (`a0 audit cloudflare`), security (`a0 audit security`), and uptime/health (`open-source/a0/src/control-plane.ts`) each exist as separate surfaces, but `a0_status`/`a0_mesh_snapshot` don't combine them — no single glanceable command.
   *Recommendation:* Add `a0 status --platform` (or extend `a0_mesh_snapshot`) to fan out to `auditCloudflare()`, `runSecurityAudit()`, and the control-plane health reader in parallel and render one-screen output.

9. **User #63 — PARTIAL.** `a0.config.json` and other a0 CLI config are read fresh (`readFileSync`) on every invocation, so CLI-side config changes apply immediately — but there's no documentation of which Claude Code session-level settings (permission mode, model, `.mcp.json` server registration) require a session restart versus apply live.
   *Recommendation:* Document explicitly (CLAUDE.md or `docs/tools`) which config surfaces are live-per-command versus restart-required, so operators aren't guessing.

10. **User #61 — PARTIAL.** `loadA0Config()` degrades gracefully to `{}`/policies-off when `a0.config.json` is absent, and global Claude settings ship sensible defaults — but there's no `a0 new <app>`/scaffold command in `open-source/a0/src/cli.ts` to bootstrap a new project with house-default config.
    *Recommendation:* Add an `a0 new <app>` scaffold that seeds a starter `a0.config.json`/wrangler config with house defaults, extending "sensible defaults" to bootstrapping, not just zero-config running.

11. **User #60 — PARTIAL.** Permission grants are inspectable as plain JSON across settings files, but there's no designed audit trail over time — only incidental crash-recovery backups (`~/.claude/backups/*.claude.json.backup.<timestamp>`).
    *Recommendation:* Add an `a0 audit permissions` command that diffs successive settings backups and git history of project `.claude/settings*.json`, rendering a chronological log of permission-rule changes.
